content:
First, note that the 3×3 grid is a bipartite graph (the usual “checkerboard’’ coloring).  One part has 5 squares (say the black squares), the other 4.  Since every edge must join an odd to an even, all 5 odd numbers must sit on one part and all 4 evens on the other.  There are exactly 2 ways to choose which part gets the odds, then 5! ways to permute the odds among the 5 chosen squares and 4! ways to permute the evens among the other 4 squares.  Hence the total number of Isthmian labellings is

Total = 2·5!·4! = 2·120·24 = 5760.

We now mod out by rotations of the square (the cyclic group C₄).  By Burnside’s Lemma the number of orbits is

(1/4)·(Fix(id) + Fix(90°) + Fix(180°) + Fix(270°)).

Clearly Fix(id)=5760.  But any nontrivial rotation has no fixed labellings with nine distinct labels (rotating by 90° forces 4‐cycles of cells to carry the same number, impossible; rotating by 180° forces 2‐cycles of cells to carry the same number, again impossible).  Thus Fix(90°)=Fix(180°)=Fix(270°)=0, and

Number of orbits = (1/4)·5760 = 1440.

Answer: \boxed{1440}.
